summary: Deep scan of React rendering, context subscriptions, missing memoization, and mount-time API calls. Found 20 issues ranging from 52 parallel settings fetches to render-path Date allocations and missing React.memo on frequently-rendered components.

findings.title: SettingsContext fires 52 parallel GET requests on mount
findings.description: SettingsProvider maps all 52 SETTING_KEYS into individual api.getAppSetting() calls via Promise.all. Each is a separate HTTP request (or IPC call in Tauri). Should use a single batch endpoint GET /settings that returns all settings at once.
findings.fix: Add GET /api/settings (no key param) that returns all settings as a JSON object. Replace the Promise.all(SETTING_KEYS.map(...)) with a single api.getAllSettings() call.

findings.title: PluginContext fires 5 parallel API calls on mount without batching
findings.description: PluginProvider calls refreshPlugins, refreshCommands, refreshStatusBar, refreshPanels, refreshViews as 5 separate API calls on mount. These could be consolidated into a single GET /plugins/state endpoint.
findings.fix: Add a single GET /api/plugins/state endpoint returning {plugins, commands, statusBarItems, panels, views}. Call it once on mount.

findings.title: AIContext.Provider value is not memoized - causes re-renders on every state change
findings.description: The AIProvider passes a fresh object literal to AIContext.Provider on every render. Since it includes 25+ properties including state like messages, isStreaming, config, any change causes all consumers to re-render. The value object should be wrapped in useMemo.
findings.fix: Wrap the value={{ ... }} object in useMemo with appropriate dependency array, similar to how TaskContext and UndoContext already do it.

findings.title: VoiceContext.Provider value is not memoized
findings.description: VoiceProvider passes a fresh object literal to VoiceContext.Provider on every render. Any state change (isListening, isSpeaking, etc.) creates a new context value and re-renders all consumers including components that only use settings or the registry.
findings.fix: Wrap the value in useMemo with dependency array covering all included values.

findings.title: PluginContext.Provider value is not memoized
findings.description: PluginProvider passes a fresh object literal on every render. The 30-second polling interval for refreshStatusBar/refreshPanels triggers re-renders that propagate to all consumers even when data hasn't changed.
findings.fix: Wrap the value in useMemo. Additionally, in refresh callbacks, compare new data with current before calling setState to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

findings.title: TaskItemContent and TaskItemActions are not wrapped in React.memo
findings.description: TaskItemContent and TaskItemActions are child components of the memoized TaskItem, but they themselves are not memoized. While TaskItem is memoized, these sub-components call useGeneralSettings() which subscribes to the entire settings context. Any settings change re-renders every visible TaskItemContent.
findings.fix: Wrap both components in React.memo. Consider extracting settings values as props from the parent TaskItem instead of subscribing to context independently.

findings.title: new Date() called in render path of TaskItem creates new object on every render
findings.description: Line 80: `new Date(task.dueDate) < new Date()` — creates two Date objects per TaskItem per render. This is computed inline in the component body (not in useMemo). For a list of 100 tasks, that's 200 unnecessary Date allocations per render cycle.
findings.fix: Compute isOverdue in a useMemo or pass it as a pre-computed prop from the parent list component.

findings.title: buildChildStats recomputed on every TaskList render without memoization
findings.description: buildChildStats(tasks) is called directly in the render body of TaskList without useMemo. It iterates all tasks to build a parent-child map. This runs on every render even when tasks haven't changed.
findings.fix: Wrap in useMemo: const childStatsMap = useMemo(() => buildChildStats(tasks), [tasks]);

findings.title: flattenVisible function recreated and called on every render
findings.description: flattenVisible is defined as a nested function inside the render body and called immediately. It depends on childStatsMap, expandedIds, and onAddSubtask. Should be memoized.
findings.fix: Use useMemo: const visibleTasks = useMemo(() => flattenVisible(topLevel, 0), [tasks, expandedIds, onAddSubtask]);

findings.title: DraggableCard in Board.tsx not memoized — new Date() in render path
findings.description: DraggableCard is a plain function component (not React.memo'd) that creates two Date objects per render on line 59. Every board re-render re-renders all cards.
findings.fix: Wrap DraggableCard in React.memo and move the isOverdue computation to useMemo.

findings.title: useAppState refetches projects/tags/blockedIds on every task count change
findings.description: The effect at line 126 uses taskCount (state.tasks.length) as a dependency, refetching projects, tags, and blocked task IDs whenever a task is added or removed. This fires 3 API calls on every task CRUD operation, even though projects/tags rarely change.
findings.fix: Remove taskCount dependency. Instead, refetch projects/tags only when a task's projectId or tags change, or rely on explicit refresh triggers after project/tag mutations.

findings.title: useSoundEffect recreates callback whenever any setting changes
findings.description: The play callback depends on the entire settings object. Any settings change (e.g., accent_color) recreates the play function, which propagates to every component using playSound as a dependency.
findings.fix: Destructure only the needed settings (sound_enabled, sound_volume, sound_complete, etc.) and use those as the dependency array instead of the full settings object.

findings.title: Today.tsx creates new Date on every render for toDateKey
findings.description: const today = toDateKey(new Date()) is called directly in the component body without useMemo. Since this value only changes once per day, it should be memoized.
findings.fix: Wrap in useMemo: const today = useMemo(() => toDateKey(new Date()), []);

findings.title: Upcoming.tsx same issue — toDateKey(new Date()) in render path
findings.description: Same as Today.tsx — creates new Date() in render body without memoization.
findings.fix: Wrap in useMemo.

findings.title: Sidebar not wrapped in React.memo despite receiving 25+ props
findings.description: Sidebar is a complex component rendered from AppLayout. It receives ~25 props and is rendered twice (desktop + mobile drawer). It uses useGeneralSettings() internally, so it re-renders on any settings change. Not memoized.
findings.fix: Wrap Sidebar in React.memo. Since it uses internal context, this will prevent re-renders from prop-only changes while still responding to settings changes.

findings.title: AppLayout creates inline arrow functions on every render for handlers
findings.description: Multiple inline arrow functions like () => setSearchOpen(true), () => setProjectModalOpen(true), () => setDrawerOpen(false) are created fresh on every render and passed as props to Sidebar and other components, defeating React.memo if applied.
findings.fix: Use useCallback for these handlers or extract them as stable references.

findings.title: useReducedMotion subscribes to SettingsContext in every TaskItem
findings.description: useReducedMotion calls useGeneralSettings() which subscribes to the full SettingsContext. Since it's used in TaskItem (memoized), CompletionBurst, and Sidebar, every settings change triggers re-renders in all these components even when reduce_animations hasn't changed.
findings.fix: Create a dedicated ReducedMotionContext that only provides the boolean value, or use a selector pattern to avoid re-renders when unrelated settings change.

findings.title: ProjectButton and ProjectTree not memoized
findings.description: ProjectTree rebuilds childrenMap on every render without memoization (lines 29-36). ProjectButton components re-render whenever any Sidebar prop changes. For users with many projects this adds unnecessary work.
findings.fix: Memoize childrenMap with useMemo. Wrap ProjectButton in React.memo.

findings.title: SettingsContext updateSetting triggers DOM manipulation inside setState
findings.description: updateSetting calls DOM-mutating functions (applyAccentColor, applyDensity, etc.) synchronously inside the setState callback. While not incorrect, it's a side effect inside a state updater which React docs advise against. It also means these DOM operations run during rendering.
findings.fix: Move the side effects to a useEffect that watches the relevant settings values, or call them after the setState call (not inside).

findings.title: TaskItem has duplicate useEffect watching task.status for same trigger
findings.description: Two separate useEffect hooks watch task.status changes — one sets animClass (line 62) and another sets showBurst (line 102). Both check the same condition (pending -> completed). They could be combined into one effect.
findings.fix: Merge the two effects into a single useEffect that handles both animClass and showBurst.

spotChecks.useAppState.ts: Round 1-2 fixes appear solid: appStateValue is properly memoized with useMemo (line 260), badge counts use useMemo (lines 210-233), visibleTasks filtered with useMemo (line 189). No regressions found.
spotChecks.TaskList.tsx: SortableTaskItem wrapped in React.memo (line 55) — correct. TaskItem also React.memo'd (line 38). However TaskList itself and its children buildChildStats/flattenVisible are NOT memoized (new findings PERF-308, PERF-309).
spotChecks.PluginContext.tsx: Has mountedRef for cleanup (line 33) — good. But provider value not memoized (new finding PERF-305) and 30s polling could be smarter.

prioritizedFixOrder: PERF-301: Batch settings endpoint (highest impact — 52 requests -> 1)
PERF-302: Batch plugin state endpoint (5 requests -> 1)
PERF-311: Remove taskCount trigger for project/tag refetch
PERF-307/313/314: Memoize Date computations in render paths
